# LinkedIn Ads for B2B in 2026: Formats, Costs & Best Practices

## Summary
Intentsify argues that in 2026, LinkedIn has evolved from a paid social tactic into a core account-influence channel. The best B2B teams use it alongside intent signals, buying group intelligence, display, CTV, and BDR outreach. The piece covers the shift from individual targeting to account-level ABM, intent-activated targeting, and a three-question measurement framework: right accounts, right engagement, right outcomes.

## Key quotations / statistics
- "LinkedIn Ads in 2026 are no longer just a paid social tactic. For B2B go-to-market teams that are serious about pipeline, LinkedIn has become a core account-influence channel."
- "The best B2B teams are now using LinkedIn as an account-influence channel — activated by real demand signals, coordinated with other channels, and measured by pipeline contribution, not clicks."
- "Target accounts, not just individuals. LinkedIn's value in B2B is its ability to reach professionals at specific companies. Build your campaigns around an account list, not a demographic profile."
- "Design campaigns for buying groups, not single personas. A campaign that reaches only the VP of Marketing at a target account hasn't reached the buying group — it's reached one member."
- "The single biggest lever B2B teams have to improve LinkedIn Ads ROI isn't creative or format selection — it's audience quality."
- "Intent-activated targeting means your LinkedIn campaigns turn on — and increase in intensity — when accounts show active research behavior relevant to your solution."
- Three measurement dimensions: account match rate + intent alignment (right accounts); multi-asset engagement + stage progression (right engagement); SQL rate + opportunity creation + pipeline contribution (right outcomes)

## Annotations for stinger-forge
- Informs `guides/02-linkedin-ads.md`: the account-level ABM framing and buying group targeting approach should be the guiding philosophy, not individual demographic targeting
- Informs `guides/01-channel-fit-diagnosis.md`: LinkedIn is positioned as demand creation + demand influence, not demand capture - this distinction matters for ICP scoring
- Key insight for `guides/00-principles.md`: measuring by pipeline contribution (SQL rate, opportunity creation) not CPL is the 2026 doctrine
- The "intent-activated" approach (only run campaigns when accounts show research signals) is a notable 2026 shift from always-on campaigns
